cnbc.title: Iran war: Trump will release 172 million barrels of oil from Strategic Petroleum Reserve
cnbc.summary: The U.S. currently has 415 million barrels of oil in its strategic reserve, about 58% of the authorized capacity of 714 million barrels.

cnbc.title: IEA agrees to release record 400 million barrels of oil to address Iran war supply disruption
cnbc.summary: The move comes as the Iran war continues to choke off traffic through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz.

cnbc.title: Iran war: Trump says he's not worried about domestic terror attack
cnbc.summary: President Donald Trump said oil companies should send tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, which has remained effectively closed due to the war on Iran.

cnbc.title: Atlassian slashes 10% of workforce to 'self-fund' investments in AI and enterprise sales
cnbc.summary: Atlassian said it's cutting 10% of its workforce, or about 1,600 jobs, as it restructures to fund investments in artificial intelligence and enterprise sales.

cnbc.title: Oracle's TikTok stake sits at just over $2 billion, filing shows
cnbc.summary: Oracle bought 15% of TikTok's U.S. operations in January after China's ByteDance was forced by U.S. law to divest.

cnbc.title: U.S. deficit tops $1 trillion through February but runs below year-ago pace
cnbc.summary: For the fiscal year to date, the deficit totaled $1.004 trillion, about 12% lower than the comparable period in 2025.

cnbc.title: Trump will decide whether U.S. participates in IEA release of oil reserves, Interior Secretary says
cnbc.summary: The IEA is group of 32 advanced economies primarily in Europe, North America and northeast Asia. The U.S. is a member of the organization.

cnbc.title: The memory stock cycle of boom-bust-repeat is over, executives say
cnbc.summary: Shares of Micron have skyrocketed 370% over the past year, while Sandisk is up more than 1100%.

cnbc.title: Five things to know about Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei
cnbc.summary: "The Iranians are showing defiance by choosing the son of Khamenei," a former diplomat told CNBC.

cnbc.title: Iran sends millions of oil barrels to China through Strait of Hormuz even as war chokes the waterway
cnbc.summary: Iran has continued to ship crude oil via the Strait of Hormuz to China even as the war between U.S.-Israel and Iran has disrupted broader energy supplies via the waterway.
